text:
Something insane happened on the Seine: the opening ceremonies of the 2024 Olympics managed to do major damage to the Olympic brand.

In case you missed it, there were several bizarre scenes, including what looked to be a depiction of the Fourth Horseman of the Revelation (see chapter 6:8). The weirdest thing was a tableau that appeared to most observers as a parody of the Last Supper, the meal Jesus shared with his apostles that Christians view as the time when he instituted the Lord’s Supper (Eucharist or Holy Communion).

While some have tried to claim that this was not a take-off on Leonardo da Vinci’s famous painting of the Last Supper, but something from Greek mythology, that seems pretty lame. When the Paris scene, which featured drag queens and children, is juxtaposed with the Da Vinci masterpiece the similarity is striking.

A spokeswoman for the International Olympic Committee (IOC) issued a half-hearted ‘apology,’ stating that it was not intended to mock anything religious and that the IOC is sorry if anyone was offended. Talk about gaslighting! These things are in the planning for years so there was lots of time to reflect on how it would come across. Anyone who thought it would not be seen as blasphemous by the world’s Christians must be living in some woke la-la land.

It’s not just the Christian Church that is angered by this display. Muslims, who revere Jesus as one of the greatest of the prophets, are also feeling insulted. In fact, many non-religious people have expressed disgust at these disrespectful antics.

Some have pointed out that that it’s anti-woman and also that the hyper-sexualized twerking and gyrating of drag queens is hardly the right atmosphere to bring children into. One of the cast actually had a ‘wardrobe malfunction’ that left his male genitalia exposed!

When is the world going to say, ‘enough is enough’? When will people see that this sort of spectacle only hurts your cause? When will the IOC and TV networks smarten up? Answer to that one: when enough advertisers pull their financial support.

We could view this descent into the mire as an expression of cultural suicide. By mocking Christianity people are turning their backs on our heritage in the Western world. Christianity had a lot to do with giving us our form of government, our laws, modern science, the status of women, and much more. Why are we so hell-bent on casting aside the moral wisdom of the last 2,000 years? Sadly, we will regret the consequences for society if we continue on this course.

Paris 2024 brings to mind Paris 1924, the Olympics in which Eric Liddell, the Scottish sprinter, represented Britain. If you remember the movie ‘Chariots of Fire’ you’ll recall that Liddell was a deeply committed Christian who, despite the pleadings of powerful people like the Prince of Wales, refused to run in one of the races that was scheduled for a Sunday. Today’s Christians (let alone the unchurched) will have no idea of what the issue was for Liddell. We have lost all concept of the Lord’s Day or Christian Sabbath, which previous generations took seriously. For Liddell, Sunday was the day of rest and worship commanded by God in the Ten Commandments (the traditional view of Presbyterians and many other denominations). He refused to compromise his conscience on the matter. He remained true to his convictions and earned the respect of his countrymen. The sequel is that he went on to become a missionary and died at age 43 in a Japanese internment camp during the Second World War. (Interestingly, his widow ended up living in Canada.)

Eric Liddell displayed integrity, a character trait that often seems to be in short supply these days. His reverence for God was so strong that he refused to dishonor his name.

Quite a contrast to what happened in Paris a hundred years later!

John Vaudry is a retired minister, living in Pembroke.

title: THE VALLEY PULPIT: Bizarre scenes part of opening ceremony of 2024 Olympics | Pembroke Observer
description: Something insane happened on the Seine: the opening ceremonies of the 2024 Olympics managed to do major damage to the Olympic brand.
